description: TypeScript ESM Node16/NodeNext fixture. Source code writes `import { Foo } from './foo.js'` per the NodeNext convention; the on-disk file is `foo.ts`. Exercises sextant's resolver rewrite from JS-family extensions to TS-family equivalents when the importer is itself TS. Pre-fix, this corpus reported 0% local import resolution; post-fix it should report 100%, and graph-based ranking should fire because resolution clears the 90% health gate.

cases.notes: Canonical schema definition. schemas.ts must rank #1 — the def site beats the consumer files via def-site signal + fan-in. Post-fix, fan-in on schemas.ts should be 2 (api.ts and index.ts both import it via './schemas.js'); pre-fix, fan-in was 0 because the resolver couldn't bridge the .js→.ts gap.
Function definition lookup. api.ts must rank #1 by def-site signal. Validates that the resolver fix doesn't regress single-file definition retrieval.

Cross-file consumer pattern. Session is defined in schemas.ts and imported by api.ts and index.ts via the NodeNext .js convention. schemas.ts must rank #1 — proves the fan-in graph edge was recorded post-fix.
Garbage query. Must return zero results regardless of resolver state.
NodeNext ESM source writes './module-config.mjs' while the implementation is module-config.mts. Retrieval must surface the .mts definition.
NodeNext CJS source writes './common-config.cjs' while the implementation is common-config.cts. Retrieval must surface the .cts definition.
